name: Mahogany Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Mahogany. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Mahogany surface cleaning without charring

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal absorption in Mahogany lignin and cellulose

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise surface cleaning control

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without wood damage

pulseWidth.description: Pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete surface treatment

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning pattern

dwellTime.description: Effective dwell time per spot for contaminant removal

power.description: Typical range for wood cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 50 W for light contamination on mahogany, max ~200 W to prevent charring (based on Nd:YAG systems for cultural heritage wood, e.g., Lee et al., 2016, J. Cult. Herit.).

scan_speed.description: Faster speeds reduce heat accumulation on heat-sensitive mahogany; 2000 mm/s optimal for light contaminants, slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy; from industry manuals like IPG Photonics laser cleaning guidelines.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for minimal heat-affected zone in wood; 20 ns balances cleaning efficiency and substrate safety, avoiding picosecond/femtosecond for cost (Verho et al., 2019, Opt. Lasers Eng.).

repetition_rate.description: 40 kHz recommended for mahogany to enable fast cleaning without excessive heat buildup; higher rates (>50 kHz) risk accumulation on low-conductivity wood (academic review in Pouli et al., 2015, Lasers Med. Sci.).

energy_density.description: Fluence below mahogany damage threshold (~2-4 J/cm² at 1064 nm); 1.5 J/cm² safe for contaminant removal without ablation (ablation studies on hardwoods, e.g., Ancona et al., 2010, Appl. Surf. Sci.).

spot_size.description: 200 μm for precise cleaning on detailed mahogany surfaces; smaller for fine work, larger for broad areas (equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for wood processing).

pass_count.description: Multi-pass (2 recommended) for complete removal on mahogany without single-pass overheating; single pass for light dirt (industry case studies on wood restoration, e.g., EU project LASER4ART).

overlap_ratio.description: 60% ensures uniform coverage on irregular wood surfaces like mahogany; 30-50% for speed, higher for heavy contamination (process optimization in Asmus, 1995, Stud. Conserv.).
